nonBlockingIssues.description: useState used as side-effect runner instead of useEffect in ConnectionsList.tsx — unconventional pattern
No transactional atomicity in exchange-token flow — partial connection possible if accountsGet fails after connection creation
N+1 query pattern in getAllConnections — separate queries for each connection's account links
institutionName used as join key for account_links instead of direct connectionId FK
Missing API route handler tests — only query-layer tests exist for sync endpoint
Non-null assertion on removed transaction_id (txn.transaction_id!) — defensive guard clause would be safer
Redundant balance updates inside pagination loop — should update once after loop completes
Duplicated CSV parser (parseSimpleRow in client vs parseCSVRow in lib/csv.ts) — bug fixes won't propagate
Missing API route handler tests — only query-layer tests exist for import endpoints
Import route doesn't validate accountId references an existing account before importing
Fuzzy substring matching for merchant rules could produce false positives with short keys
Already-categorized transactions silently dropped when passed via explicit IDs (not via all=true)
max_tokens: 2048 could truncate AI response for large transaction batches (100+)
Auto-create merchant rule test tests query function directly rather than through PUT /api/transactions/[id]

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md project structure to include new files from integrations milestone: sandbox-connect route, import routes, merchant-rules routes, sync.ts and imports.ts query files, categorize.ts/csv.ts/encryption.ts/openai.ts lib files
Added 'Merchant Rule Matching' section to architecture.md documenting fuzzy substring matching behavior
Added 'OpenAI Integration Pattern' section to architecture.md documenting prompt structure, response parsing, error handling, and max_tokens considerations

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: CRITICAL: Correct the Plaid amount sign guidance. Current text says 'CRITICAL - Amount signs are INVERTED: Positive = debit (money out), Negative = credit (money in). Flip signs when storing.' This is WRONG — the codebase convention (positive = expense, negative = income) already matches Plaid's convention (positive = debit, negative = credit). Remove the 'Flip signs when storing' instruction. Change to: 'Plaid positive = expense (money out), Plaid negative = income (money in). This matches our storage convention — store as-is, no sign flip needed.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: src/db/schema.ts:44 comment: 'cents (positive = expense, negative = income)'. Plaid convention: positive = debit. plaid-transaction-sync worker correctly did NOT flip signs. Test at plaid-sync.test.ts:116 confirms $25.50 stored as +2550 cents.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the .env.local boundary. Current text says 'Do NOT modify .env.local'. Workers implementing features that require new environment variables (e.g., PLAID_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY for token encryption) need to add them. Suggest: 'Do NOT modify existing values in .env.local. Workers may ADD new required environment variables with a note in discoveredIssues.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: plaid-link-and-connections worker needed to add PLAID_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY for AES-256-GCM encryption. Worker reported this as a discovered issue since it technically violated the boundary.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce the 'test both layers' convention with stronger language. Multiple features (plaid-transaction-sync, csv-import, ai-auto-categorization) only test the DB query layer but not the API route handlers, despite AGENTS.md explicitly requiring both. Consider: 'MANDATORY: Every feature with API endpoints MUST include tests for both the database query layer AND the API route handlers. Route-level tests catch validation logic, dollar-to-cent conversion, and error responses that query-layer tests miss. PRs missing route-level tests will be flagged in review.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 3 out of 4 features in this milestone (plaid-transaction-sync, csv-import, ai-auto-categorization) lack API route handler tests despite AGENTS.md explicitly stating this requirement. This is a systemic pattern where workers test query functions but skip route handlers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document preferred data-fetching-on-mount pattern. ConnectionsList.tsx uses useState(() => { fetch() }) while PlaidLinkButton.tsx uses useEffect(() => { fetch() }, []). Recommend standardizing on useEffect for data fetching on mount, as useState initializer is synchronous and not the idiomatic React pattern for side effects.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: ConnectionsList.tsx:86 vs PlaidLinkButton.tsx:48 use different patterns for the same purpose within the same milestone.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add agent-browser guidance for cross-origin iframe interaction. Plaid Link opens in a cross-origin iframe where coordinate-based clicking doesn't work. Workers testing Plaid flows should use the sandbox API endpoint for programmatic testing rather than attempting to interact with the Plaid Link iframe via agent-browser.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: plaid-transaction-sync worker's skill feedback suggests this based on experience testing Plaid sandbox flows.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature description for plaid-transaction-sync says 'negate for storage' which contradicts actual correct behavior
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate — same issue as the AGENTS.md Plaid sign flip guidance already captured in suggestedGuidanceUpdates
